Home / #The Lounge SOLVED ✓ SOLVED dtest Opened by hperkins · 2 replies ★ Starred WORKFLOW Status: Solved Topic status Open Needs answer Solved Decision made Archived Update status Snooze Clear snooze Later today Tomorrow Next week Snooze Assign STATUS HISTORY Add poll Split or merge topic Curate topic memory Refresh living brief Not enough eligible posts for automatic refresh Summary Bold Italic Strike Code Spoiler Quote Heading List Code block Link Emoji Source Source post IDs Publish summary 0 / 20000 Discard draft Add related topic HP ✦ 0 COMMENDS hperkins OP STAFF Jul 12, 2026 at 02:10 UTC TEsttt ＋ Edit Delete topic HP ✦ 0 COMMENDS MARKED AS THE ANSWER ✦ hperkins STAFF Jul 12, 2026 at 03:18 UTC Cloudflare-backed delivery - Developer enablement - Customer-facing technical implementation - Solutions Engineer positioning Likely priority URLs: - / - /ai-enablement/ - /work/ - /work/flavor-agent/ - /work/flavor-agent/ai-governance/ - /work/flavor-agent/demo/ - /work/upstream-core-ai-stack/ - /work/ai-provider-for-codex/ - /work/dj-lee-voices-of-judah/ - /about/ - /contact/ - /how-this-was-built/ Investigate hperkins.com separately. Determine whether the two domains: - Compete for the same branded searches - Overlap on non-branded topics - Duplicate professional positioning - Contain conflicting About or résumé information - Fragment public links or entity signals - Use inconsistent canonicals - Serve genuinely different audiences or demonstrations Do not recommend a migration, domain-wide redirect, or cross-domain canonicalization without evidence. Where backlink data is not publicly available, classify backlink-fragmentation conclusions [PRIVATE DATA] or clearly identify the third-party estimate used. PRIORITIZATION MODEL Use: P0 A confirmed issue materially blocking availability, crawling, indexability, canonical consolidation, or a primary conversion. P1 A high-confidence issue with substantial likely effect on qualified discoverability, page interpretation, or conversion. P2 A meaningful improvement with moderate expected effect, narrower reach, or a dependency on other work. P3 A low-risk refinement, housekeeping item, or presentational improvement. For every recommendation, provide: - Priority - Evidence state - Expected impact: High, Medium, or Low - Confidence: High, Medium, or Low - Implementation effort: Small, Medium, or Large - Implementation risk or dependency - Responsible layer Do not use numerical traffic or ranking forecasts unless supported by actual data. REQUIRED DELIVERABLES Produce the following sections in this order. 1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY Include: - Audit date - Crawl and research coverage - Material tool or data limitations - What the site currently communicates to search engines and visitors - The five highest-impact SEO opportunities - The five strongest existing assets to preserve - The largest technical or content risk - Recommended primary homepage positioning - A concise explanation of what remains dependent on private data 2. TECHNICAL SEO AUDIT Create a prioritized table containing: - Priority - Confidence - Evidence state - Finding - Affected URL or template - Exact evidence - SEO or conversion consequence - Exact recommended fix - Expected impact - Implementation effort - Risk or dependency - Responsible layer Responsible layers may include: - WordPress Settings - Page editor - Block template - Classic theme template - theme.json - Functionality plugin - SEO plugin - Server configuration - Hosting panel - DNS - CDN or Cloudflare - Media library Check specifically for: - Availability and status-code errors - Redirect chains and loops - HTTPS and hostname consistency - Trailing-slash consistency - Indexation directives - X-Robots-Tag behavior - Incorrect, missing, or conflicting canonicals - Sitemap completeness and quality - robots.txt behavior - Thin, duplicate, or purposeless archives - Search-result and attachment-page exposure - Category, tag, author, date, pagination, and feed handling - Broken internal and external links - Soft 404s - Duplicate, missing, or weak title tags - Duplicate, missing, or weak meta descriptions - Missing, repeated, or unclear H1 headings - JavaScript-dependent primary content - Orphan or weakly linked pages - Breadcrumbs - Mobile rendering - Layout shift - Slow images, fonts, scripts, and stylesheets - Render-blocking assets - Unnecessary page-level plugin assets - Compression, caching, and CDN behavior where observable - WebP or AVIF opportunities - Missing image dimensions - Appropriate lazy loading - Accessibility issues directly affecting content access or navigation - Security headers only where they have a genuine availability, rendering, trust, or performance implication Do not recommend noindexing, consolidation, or deletion solely because a page has a low word count. 3. INDEXABLE PAGE INVENTORY Provide a full table for every discovered indexable or potentially indexable HTML page. Include: - URL - Page type - HTTP status - Crawlability - Current indexing directive - Declared canonical - Sitemap inclusion - Current purpose - Intended audience - Current search intent - Recommended dominant topic - Secondary topics - Conversion goal - Keep, consolidate, rewrite, redirect, noindex, or remove - Evidence state For content depth, consider: - Approximate word count - Number and quality of substantive sections - Code or technical examples - Demonstrations - Screenshots or diagrams - Release evidence - Pull requests, repositories, or external artifacts Explain every redirect, consolidation, removal, and noindex recommendation. Do not remove or noindex a useful page merely because it contains little prose. If the inventory is too large for the main report, place the complete inventory in an appendix or exportable table and summarize template-level patterns in this section. 4. PAGE-BY-PAGE ON-PAGE PLAN For every confirmed priority page, provide: - Current title tag, or “missing” - Proposed title tag - Approximate proposed-title character count - Current meta description, or “missing” - Proposed meta description - Approximate proposed-description character count - Current H1 - Proposed H1, only where a change is justified - Dominant search intent - Primary topic - A drafted or closely specified introduction - Recommended H2 and H3 structure - Missing supporting sections - Contextual internal links to add - Destination URL - Suggested anchor text - Recommended CTA - CTA placement - Informative-image and media recommendations Write exact proposed title tags and meta descriptions. Keep them concise and SERP-aware, but do not sacrifice accuracy or clarity to arbitrary character limits. Preserve correct project names and technical terminology. Do not rewrite strong existing copy without a clear search-intent, comprehension, or conversion rationale. 5. HOMEPAGE POSITIONING Evaluate whether the homepage should primarily position Henry Perkins as: - A WordPress AI and Solutions Engineer - A governed AI workflow specialist - A customer-facing WordPress implementation engineer - Another evidence-supported formulation Base the decision on: - Visible project evidence - Likely hiring and commercial intent - Search-result composition - Differentiation from generic WordPress positioning - The relationship between the homepage and deeper case studies Provide: - Recommended homepage title tag - Recommended meta description - Recommended H1 - Revised hero paragraph - Primary CTA - Secondary CTA - Recommended page-section order - Proof artifacts that should appear above or near the fold - Search topics the homepage should intentionally not target The revised hero must preserve the evidence-first voice and the “trust is structural” thesis. ＋ Edit Delete HP ✦ 0 COMMENDS hperkins STAFF Jul 12, 2026 at 07:49 UTC Test

HP ✦0 Commends

Marked as the answer✦

hperkins Staff Jul 12, 2026 at 03:18 UTC

Cloudflare-backed delivery

- Developer enablement

- Customer-facing technical implementation

- Solutions Engineer positioning

Likely priority URLs:

- /

- /ai-enablement/

- /work/

- /work/flavor-agent/

- /work/flavor-agent/ai-governance/

- /work/flavor-agent/demo/

- /work/upstream-core-ai-stack/

- /work/ai-provider-for-codex/

- /work/dj-lee-voices-of-judah/

- /about/

- /contact/

- /how-this-was-built/

Investigate hperkins.com separately.

Determine whether the two domains:

- Compete for the same branded searches

- Overlap on non-branded topics

- Duplicate professional positioning

- Contain conflicting About or résumé information

- Fragment public links or entity signals

- Use inconsistent canonicals

- Serve genuinely different audiences or demonstrations

Do not recommend a migration, domain-wide redirect, or cross-domain canonicalization without evidence.

Where backlink data is not publicly available, classify backlink-fragmentation conclusions [PRIVATE DATA] or clearly identify the third-party estimate used.

PRIORITIZATION MODEL

Use:

P0

A confirmed issue materially blocking availability, crawling, indexability, canonical consolidation, or a primary conversion.

P1

A high-confidence issue with substantial likely effect on qualified discoverability, page interpretation, or conversion.

P2

A meaningful improvement with moderate expected effect, narrower reach, or a dependency on other work.

P3

A low-risk refinement, housekeeping item, or presentational improvement.

For every recommendation, provide:

- Priority

- Evidence state

- Expected impact: High, Medium, or Low

- Confidence: High, Medium, or Low

- Implementation effort: Small, Medium, or Large

- Implementation risk or dependency

- Responsible layer

Do not use numerical traffic or ranking forecasts unless supported by actual data.

REQUIRED DELIVERABLES

Produce the following sections in this order.

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Include:

- Audit date

- Crawl and research coverage

- Material tool or data limitations

- What the site currently communicates to search engines and visitors

- The five highest-impact SEO opportunities

- The five strongest existing assets to preserve

- The largest technical or content risk

- Recommended primary homepage positioning

- A concise explanation of what remains dependent on private data

2. TECHNICAL SEO AUDIT

Create a prioritized table containing:

- Priority

- Confidence

- Evidence state

- Finding

- Affected URL or template

- Exact evidence

- SEO or conversion consequence

- Exact recommended fix

- Expected impact

- Implementation effort

- Risk or dependency

- Responsible layer

Responsible layers may include:

- WordPress Settings

- Page editor

- Block template

- Classic theme template

- theme.json

- Functionality plugin

- SEO plugin

- Server configuration

- Hosting panel

- DNS

- CDN or Cloudflare

- Media library

Check specifically for:

- Availability and status-code errors

- Redirect chains and loops

- HTTPS and hostname consistency

- Trailing-slash consistency

- Indexation directives

- X-Robots-Tag behavior

- Incorrect, missing, or conflicting canonicals

- Sitemap completeness and quality

- robots.txt behavior

- Thin, duplicate, or purposeless archives

- Search-result and attachment-page exposure

- Category, tag, author, date, pagination, and feed handling

- Broken internal and external links

- Soft 404s

- Duplicate, missing, or weak title tags

- Duplicate, missing, or weak meta descriptions

- Missing, repeated, or unclear H1 headings

- JavaScript-dependent primary content

- Orphan or weakly linked pages

- Breadcrumbs

- Mobile rendering

- Layout shift

- Slow images, fonts, scripts, and stylesheets

- Render-blocking assets

- Unnecessary page-level plugin assets

- Compression, caching, and CDN behavior where observable

- WebP or AVIF opportunities

- Missing image dimensions

- Appropriate lazy loading

- Accessibility issues directly affecting content access or navigation

- Security headers only where they have a genuine availability, rendering, trust, or performance implication

Do not recommend noindexing, consolidation, or deletion solely because a page has a low word count.

3. INDEXABLE PAGE INVENTORY

Provide a full table for every discovered indexable or potentially indexable HTML page.

Include:

- URL

- Page type

- HTTP status

- Crawlability

- Current indexing directive

- Declared canonical

- Sitemap inclusion

- Current purpose

- Intended audience

- Current search intent

- Recommended dominant topic

- Secondary topics

- Conversion goal

- Keep, consolidate, rewrite, redirect, noindex, or remove

- Evidence state

For content depth, consider:

- Approximate word count

- Number and quality of substantive sections

- Code or technical examples

- Demonstrations

- Screenshots or diagrams

- Release evidence

- Pull requests, repositories, or external artifacts

Explain every redirect, consolidation, removal, and noindex recommendation.

Do not remove or noindex a useful page merely because it contains little prose.

If the inventory is too large for the main report, place the complete inventory in an appendix or exportable table and summarize template-level patterns in this section.

4. PAGE-BY-PAGE ON-PAGE PLAN

For every confirmed priority page, provide:

- Current title tag, or “missing”

- Proposed title tag

- Approximate proposed-title character count

- Current meta description, or “missing”

- Proposed meta description

- Approximate proposed-description character count

- Current H1

- Proposed H1, only where a change is justified

- Dominant search intent

- Primary topic

- A drafted or closely specified introduction

- Recommended H2 and H3 structure

- Missing supporting sections

- Contextual internal links to add

- Destination URL

- Suggested anchor text

- Recommended CTA

- CTA placement

- Informative-image and media recommendations

Write exact proposed title tags and meta descriptions.

Keep them concise and SERP-aware, but do not sacrifice accuracy or clarity to arbitrary character limits.

Preserve correct project names and technical terminology.

Do not rewrite strong existing copy without a clear search-intent, comprehension, or conversion rationale.

5. HOMEPAGE POSITIONING

Evaluate whether the homepage should primarily position Henry Perkins as:

- A WordPress AI and Solutions Engineer

- A governed AI workflow specialist

- A customer-facing WordPress implementation engineer

- Another evidence-supported formulation

Base the decision on:

- Visible project evidence

- Likely hiring and commercial intent

- Search-result composition

- Differentiation from generic WordPress positioning

- The relationship between the homepage and deeper case studies

Provide:

- Recommended homepage title tag

- Recommended meta description

- Recommended H1

- Revised hero paragraph

- Primary CTA

- Secondary CTA

- Recommended page-section order

- Proof artifacts that should appear above or near the fold

- Search topics the homepage should intentionally not target

The revised hero must preserve the evidence-first voice and the “trust is structural” thesis.
